rationale: Both slides are correct: navy #14324F with bold white 'THE PROBLEM', green slide with 'THE SOLUTION' in matching styling, and an orange #FF5C39 vertical bar that sweeps left-to-right with the green content revealed behind it and the old slide intact ahead of it — mechanically coherent, no flash frames, and slide B's title only appears after the bar passes center. The sweep pacing reads back-loaded rather than a symmetric ease-in-out (the bar is only ~15% across at tile 5 / ~1.8s then nearly off-frame by 2.0s), and frame 0 shows an essentially blank slide with a stray orange dot artifact (the underline scaling from zero), which also reappears under the fading title at 50%. Slide B's title settles in through a washed-out gray before reaching white, which is acceptable as a settle but slightly cheap-looking. Otherwise composition is centered and balanced with clean bold type; one light revision pass (sweep easing, dot artifact) would make it shippable.
